meta.title: Per-step both-role figure charts (WDSF-first, researched)
meta.note: Real International Standard technique, charted per figure from the official WDSF Technique Books (2nd ed., May 2013) as the primary source (owner-provided copies; cell-level transcription, adversarially verified against the page images); dancecentral.info remains the source for figures the books do not contain. NO fabrication: figures without a verifiable per-step source were removed from the catalog rather than guessed. Each entry records its source. Turn amounts are recorded on the step row where the book prints them (WDSF prints 'x between N-1 and N' on row N, so alignment(N) = alignment(N-1) + turn(N)). Shared rise is taken from the Man's Rise-and-Fall column; a materially different Lady rise is recorded in the entry note. The books' Rotation and head-position ('Extension') columns are charted VERBATIM as the free-text `rotation` and `head` kinds (per role); rotation cells mentioning 'with shoulders' additionally map to bodyActions CBM. Role-asymmetric charts keep every printed step: the count grid is the union of both roles' printed beats, and a count one role does not dance simply has no entry for that role. The only WDSF column not modelled is Tango's 'Sudden Movement' (pressure). Regenerate packages/domain/src/figure-charts.generated.ts with scripts/gen-figure-charts.mjs. Further conventions: 'Com./Cont. to turn' prose carries no token and no note (implied by the following step's amount); a cell printing several amounts ('X then Y (Pivot/Swivel)') carries their SUM plus a verbatim note; 'X or Y' alternatives keep the directly-charted amount plus a verbatim note; a cell spanning several rows ('between/on/over steps N and M') is recorded once, on the span's last row; a fraction printed without L/R takes its direction from the partner's cell for the same step; body-turn-only cells become notes; placement qualifiers (without weight, part weight, brush, heel pull, toe pivot, shoulder leading) and unusual printed foot actions are echoed into the entry note; foot annotations '(LF)/(RF)' are dropped (feet are never stored).

figures.note: leader step 2: WDSF charts "3/8 to L between 1 and 2 Body turns slightly less". leader step 3: WDSF charts "Body continues to turn to L on 3". WDSF NOTE - Couple Position: May start in Promenade Position WDSF NOTE - Foot Placement: On step 3 the alternative foot position may be "Weight on RF". The Foot Position will depend on the type of figure following the Left Whisk. WDSF NOTE - General: Only step 3 may be used (timing 1) following figures like Outside Change, Reverse Turning Lock etc. WDSF NOTE - Exit: To exit and connect to a suitable following figure the Man untwists to R (1 2 3) to end with feet parallel with weight on RF in Closed Position. The Lady dances 4 steps around the Man starting with RF to end in Closed Position with weight on LF. (Timing 1 2 &3 or 1 & 2 3). The following step for the Man will be RF fwd in Closed Position or RF bwd in CBMP Outside Partner. When the Left Whisk is danced on 1 the timing will change. The Man untwists (timing 2 3) or makes two steps (LF in place, RF to side) to end in Closed Position, while the lady dances 4 steps (timing 2 & 3 &) or 2 steps (timing 2 3 or & 23).
